acceptance_targets: [AWARD_PAYLOAD_STOPS_AT_DIAGNOSIS] Reading the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:107; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:167; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:171; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:179; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:185, a reviewer can point to one irreversible on-page consequence beyond diagnosis alone: a lockdown already initiated, a new authority pressure entering the room, or a materially altered Kai/Yusuf relationship that was not present at the start of the scene. If the chapter still ends with only the virus named and a spoken plan to act later, it fails.

[CRAFT_CONVENIENT_NETWORK_REVEAL] From the revised passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:143; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:149; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:153; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:159, a cold reader can answer three questions from the page alone: what exact anomaly Yusuf first saw in Kai's logs, why that anomaly made him check shared handshake records, and why the facility had not already caught the spread. If any of those links still requires outside inference, it fails.

[PROSE_FRAGMENT_EMPHASIS_TIC] Across the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:15; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:21; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:35; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:45; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:55; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:161, no run longer than two stand-alone fragment paragraphs remains, and every surviving fragment introduces a new physical datum or changed state. If three or more successive fragment-only beats still function as interchangeable drum hits, it fails.
[RED-005] Ch5 track entry does not re-inventory rubber smell, ceiling height, acoustic dampening, or footfall echo as spatial information.
[RED-019] Ch5 'He'-opener rate is below 8% and 'His'-opener rate is below 7%.

findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:107; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:167; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:171; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:179; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:185, a reviewer can point to one irreversible on-page consequence beyond diagnosis alone: a lockdown already initiated, a new authority pressure entering the room, or a materially altered Kai/Yusuf relationship that was not present at the start of the scene. If the chapter still ends with only the virus named and a spoken plan to act later, it fails.
findings.problem: The chapter's first half lands the bodily horror, but the aftermath scene mostly certifies the plot turn and exits. Once Yusuf names the spread, the chapter does not force a second on-page consequence in relationship, authority, or public pressure; it ends on a spoken plan and Kai's fear, which is effective but not enough to make the back half feel shortlist-level rather than efficient.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the bedside sequence around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:107; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:149; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:167; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:171; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:179; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:185 so the diagnosis triggers one further dramatic turn on the page, not just a later promise. Options include an immediate lockdown action, an interruption by Petra or facility staff, or a sharper visible break in Kai and Yusuf's trust, but the revised scene must still preserve Kai's private fear at the close.

findings.acceptance_test: From the revised passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:143; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:149; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:153; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:159, a cold reader can answer three questions from the page alone: what exact anomaly Yusuf first saw in Kai's logs, why that anomaly made him check shared handshake records, and why the facility had not already caught the spread. If any of those links still requires outside inference, it fails.
findings.problem: Yusuf's discovery of network-wide corruption reads as too much plot payoff from too little visible procedure. The scene shows him jumping from Kai's bedside logs to shared server records and immediately finding the repeated hash, but it does not make clear what anomaly in Kai's data prompted that search, why this comparison is manual rather than automatically flagged, or what uncertainty still separates a repeated package signature from the full worst-case blackout risk.
findings.rewrite_direction: Clarify the operational chain at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:143; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:147; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:149; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:153; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:159. Give the reader the specific trigger in Kai's readout that sends Yusuf to the handshake logs, define what the repeated hash and sensory fault signature do and do not prove yet, and state why routine monitoring or prior calibration review had not already surfaced the same pattern.

findings.acceptance_test: Across the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:15; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:21; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:35; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:45; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:55; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:161, no run longer than two stand-alone fragment paragraphs remains, and every surviving fragment introduces a new physical datum or changed state. If three or more successive fragment-only beats still function as interchangeable drum hits, it fails.
findings.problem: The chapter leans too often on the same emphasis scaffold: short count fragments, blunt declarative beat, then another isolated fragment paragraph. In the crash run and again at the reveal scene, that rhythm starts to feel like a reusable intensity template rather than syntax discovered by this specific pressure, which flattens prose that is otherwise tactile and strong.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:15; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:17; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:19; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:21; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:35; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:45; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:49; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:55; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:161. Keep the compression, but absorb some fragment beats into fuller sentences and reserve stand-alone fragments for moments that add a fresh physical datum or changed state instead of merely striking the same emphasis note again.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch5 track entry does not re-inventory rubber smell, ceiling height, acoustic dampening, or footfall echo as spatial information.
findings.evidence: Ch1 line 41: 'indoor track was a 200-meter oval under high ceilings with acoustic dampening that didn't quite work. Footfalls carried, each one a clean report off the rubber surface followed by a fast, shallow echo… smelled of rubber'; Ch5 line 743: 'rubber smell of the surface, the dry high-altitude air… overhead lights throwing a flat white glare across the oval. The ceilings were high and packed with acoustic dampening, which took the sharp edge off footfalls without killing the echo'
findings.problem: Indoor track sensory inventory re-described from scratch in Ch5 after full establishment in Ch1. Same elements repeated: rubber smell, oval shape, high ceilings, acoustic dampening, footfall echo. Four chapters apart but the same space entered through the same checklist.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch5, cut the track's sensory inventory at line 743. Enter through Kai's race-specific state — starting blocks, ankle click, the synthetic muscle tightening — which the chapter already does well at line 741. The rubber/ceiling/echo package can go.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch5 'He'-opener rate is below 8% and 'His'-opener rate is below 7%.
findings.evidence: Ch5: 26 of 225 sentences (11.6%) begin with 'He'; 24 (10.7%) begin with 'His'. Combined 'He'/'His' opener rate: 22.2%. Manuscript-wide 'He' rate: 7.1%.
findings.problem: 'He' and 'His' together open more than one in five sentences in Ch5 — a Kai-POV action chapter. The pronoun saturation is particularly dense during the crash sequence, where nearly every sentence of interiority opens with 'He' or 'His'.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch5, convert at least 10 'He'-opened and 10 'His'-opened sentences to constructions that lead with the sensation, the body part, or the environment rather than the pronoun.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 4000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
